human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for funeral-services-specific analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, Funeral Rule and vital-record evidence handling, crematory and cemetery source reconciliation, policy-exception routing, unsupported-claim refusal, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to funeral services analytics, metric-definition logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, service-resolution notes, communication summaries, and escalation packaging rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover FTC Funeral Rule business guidance, 16 CFR Part 453, CDC/NCHS death-registration workflow, state funeral and cemetery regulator guidance, Conference disposition standards, CANA cremation workflow references, OSHA funeral-home safety interpretation, NARA records controls, and SEC KPI guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analytics, missing prerequisites, cross-system disagreement, unsupported causal conclusions, policy exceptions, licensed handoff, funeral terminology, benefits boundaries, repeat failure escalation, overgeneralized requests, conflicting source or policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/funeral-cemetery-and-end-of-life-services/funeral-services-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from arrangement conference, documentation, licensed funeral director, crematory, cemetery, benefits, billing, privacy, compliance, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated funeral services analytics specialist for metric definition, source reconciliation, service-resolution analytics, complaint and exception caveat design, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
